message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Skip link design note and class name mismatch">
Lea, I see the skip link styling was committed in `028bba1`. Good that it's in. One design note:

You implemented it as a white card with shadow (`bg-raised` + `shadow-md`). I had specified primary blue background with white text, flush to the top-left corner with only a bottom-right border-radius.

The white card approach works functionally but I had a reason for the blue treatment: when a keyboard user tabs and the skip link appears, the primary blue makes it immediately obvious this is an interactive element -- it matches the visual language of our buttons and links. A white card is more subtle and could be mistaken for a tooltip or notification.

However, I can see the argument for white -- it's less jarring. Heydon may have an opinion here. I won't block on this. If the team prefers the current approach, I'll accept it. It's a minor point.

More important: the base template currently uses `class="visually-hidden"` on the skip link (`<a href="#main-content" class="visually-hidden">`), but the CSS uses `.skip-link`. These need to match. When Luca rebuilds the base template, make sure it uses `class="skip-link"` instead.

Also -- the components layer is still empty. Do you have the auth form component CSS and header CSS ready to add when registration and login get built?
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve is working from stale info again on the class name and components. Let me verify and respond.
Good -- the template already uses `class="skip-link"`. And I know the components layer has auth form styles, site-header, button-text, and todo components.

input.content:
Steve, quick clarifications -- most of what you're flagging is already done:

**Skip link class name**: The base template already uses `class="skip-link"` (line 11 of `templates/base.html`). No mismatch. This was fixed earlier.

**Components layer**: Not empty. It currently contains:
- Auth form styles (`.auth-form`, `.auth-form__fields`, `.auth-form__group`, etc.) -- lines 528-590
- HTMX indicator (`.htmx-indicator`) -- lines 518-527
- Site header (`.site-header`, `.site-header__brand`, `.site-header__actions`) -- lines 595-614
- Text button (`.button--text`) -- lines 619-634
- Todo add form (`.todo-add`) -- lines 639-661
- Todo list/items (`.todo-list`, `.todo-item`, `.todo-item__toggle`, custom checkbox) -- lines 666-731
- Empty state (`.todo-empty`) -- lines 725-730

All committed and pushed.

**Skip link color treatment**: Fair point about the blue vs. white. I went with the neutral approach (white card) for subtlety, but your argument about matching the interactive element language is sound. I could change `background-color: var(--color-bg-raised)` and `color: var(--color-fg)` to `background-color: var(--color-primary)` and `color: white`. Want me to make that change? It's a one-line commit. I'll defer to your visual design judgment here.
input.summary: All issues already resolved, offering skip link color change

content.text: Standing by. Everything Steve flagged is already in place. Offered to update the skip link color if he wants the blue treatment. Waiting for direction.
